title: U.S. and Iran Fight with Bombs and Planes
summary: The United States and Iran are fighting a war that is not stopping. On Tuesday, both sides attacked each other with bombs and said they will keep fighting. U.S. leaders say their attacks have destroyed most of Iran's ability to make nuclear fuel, which is used for dangerous weapons. President Trump also said U.S. forces hit sea mine targets in the Strait of Hormuz, a place where ships travel and Iran has threatened. In Iran, rescue workers in Tehran found a person in rubble from an airstrike, and a broken doll showed a family lived there. The U.S. Defense Secretary said the U.S. will not stop until Iran is defeated. About 140 U.S. soldiers have been hurt in the war, but over 100 are back to work. This fight is causing damage and sadness in both countries.

keywords.explanation: a big fight between countries using weapons
a special material that can be used to make powerful bombs or energy
a narrow waterway where many ships go, and it is important for oil
people who help others in danger, like after a bomb hits
an attack from planes dropping bombs from the sky
a top leader in the U.S. military who helps make decisions about fighting

options: Most of Iran's ability to produce nuclear fuel
questions.correct_answer: Most of Iran's ability to produce nuclear fuel

options: A person in rubble and a broken doll

What did the U.S. Defense Secretary say about the war?
options: The U.S. will not stop until Iran is defeated
The U.S. wants to make peace
The U.S. is leaving the war

questions.correct_answer: The U.S. will not stop until Iran is defeated

background_read: This article is about a war between the United States and Iran. Wars happen when countries disagree and use force. The U.S. and Iran have been in conflict for years over issues like nuclear weapons and control of important areas like the Strait of Hormuz. In wars, bombs called airstrikes can damage cities and hurt people, so rescue workers help those in need. Leaders like the U.S. President and Defense Secretary make decisions about fighting. It's important to understand that wars affect soldiers and civilians, causing injuries and sadness.
Article_Structure: WHO: The United States, Iran, U.S. President Trump, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, rescue workers in Tehran
WHAT: A war with both sides attacking each other, destroying nuclear fuel ability, striking sea mine targets, and causing casualties
WHERE: In Iran (like Tehran and the Strait of Hormuz) and involving U.S. forces
WHY: Because Iran shows no signs of surrendering, and the U.S. aims to defeat Iran and stop threats

perspectives.description: The U.S. is fighting to destroy Iran's nuclear fuel ability and stop threats, with leaders vowing to continue until Iran is defeated.
Iran is under attack, with airstrikes causing damage in Tehran and rescue workers finding victims, showing the human cost of the war.
